title: To amend the Intermodal Surface Transportation Efficiency Act of 1991 to designate a portion of United States Route 74 in North Carolina as a future interstate, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill changes an old transportation law from 1991 to give a special designation to a section of US Route 74 in North Carolina. The section runs from Columbus to Kings Mountain in North Carolina. The bill makes this highway section both a high priority corridor and a future interstate highway. This means the road will get special attention and funding to eventually become part of the interstate highway system.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Highways and Transit.

sections.title: Section 1 Designation of Future Interstate
sections.text: This section changes an existing transportation law from nineteen ninety-one called the Intermodal Surface Transportation Efficiency Act. The changes will add a section of United States Route seventy-four in North Carolina to two important lists. First, it adds the highway section from Columbus, North Carolina to Kings Mountain, North Carolina to the list of high priority corridors. These are highways that the government considers especially important for transportation. Second, it adds this same highway section to the list of future interstates. This means the government plans to eventually upgrade this part of Route seventy-four to meet interstate highway standards, which are higher quality roads with better safety features and design.
